events.title: Introduction to Crystal Singing Bowls
events.description: Most people have heard a crystal singing bowl and felt something they couldn't explain. This class is where that changes. Experience the resonance of frosted and gemstone-infused bowls through guided active listening — and leave understanding exactly why sound moves through the body the way it does.
events.blurb: Listen to crystal bowls and learn how frosted versus gemstone ones affect your body.

events.title: Full Immersion Sound Bath

events.title: CrossFit Games
events.address: 525 W Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95113
events.description: Parking for the 2026 CrossFit Games Attending Multiple Days? The 3-Day Parking Pass is the recommended option for guests attending more than one day of the CrossFit Games. Enjoy the convenience of a single parking purchase for the entire weekend, guaranteed access to the ABC Lot, and the flexibility to come and go as needed throughout the event. 3-Day Parking Pass (ABC Lot) Guaranteed parking access in the ABC Lot all weekend Unlimited in-and-out privileges throughout the event One parking credential valid for all three event days No need to purchase parking separately each day Exclusive access to the ABC Lot Pricing Advance Purchase: $150 + applicable fees Event-Day Rate (if available): $180 Please note: ABC Lot inventory is limited and may sell out before the event. Advance purchase is strongly recommended. Purchase All 3 Days Vip Parking Package (includes In and Out Privileges in the A-B-C Main Lot) Attending a Single Day? Single-day parking is available in the Delmas Lots. Single-Day Parking Pass (Delmas Lots) Valid only on the selected event day Parking access from 8 AM to 7 PM No in-and-out privileges Re-entry requires a new parking purchase Pricing Advance Purchase: $45 + applicable fees Event-Day Rate: $50 Purchase General Event Parking for Friday, 7/24 Purchase General Event Parking for Saturday, 7/25 Purchase General Event Parking for Sunday 7/26 Why Purchase Parking in Advance? Parking inventory for the CrossFit Games weekend is limited. Purchasing parking before arriving is the easiest way to ensure a smooth and stress-free event experience. Benefits of purchasing in advance: Secure your parking before lots reach capacity Avoid searching for available parking on event day Speed up your arrival experience Guarantee access to your selected parking option Guests attending multiple days are encouraged to purchase the 3-Day Parking Pass early, as ABC Lot inventory is limited.
events.blurb: Watch elite athletes compete in the CrossFit Games.

Parking inventory for the CrossFit Games weekend is limited. Purchasing parking before arriving is the easiest way to ensure a smooth and stress-free event experience. Benefits of purchasing in advance: Secure your parking before lots reach capacity Avoid searching for available parking on event day Speed up your arrival experience Guarantee access to your selected parking option Guests attending multiple days are encouraged to purchase the 3-Day Parking Pass early, as ABC Lot inventory is limited.

events.title: Middle School Bike Skills
events.address: Cupertino Senior Center, 21251 Stevens Creek Blvd, 95014
events.description: Middle School Bike Skills workshops prepare rising 6th, 7th, and 8th graders to bike to middle school and beyond. The original course teaches students and their parent or guardian safe biking fundamentals. New this year is a sequel which teaches students how to adapt those skills to stay safe while riding an e-bike and a third course teaching students the basics of maintaining and doing simple repairs on their own bikes. Msbs 101 - Middle School Bike Workshop Msbs 102 - e-Bike Workshop Msbs 103 - Bike Maintenance and Repair Workshop This Middle School Bike Skills program is brought to you by Cupertino Safe Routes to School in partnership with Wheel Kids. Registration is required. Links and more information can be found at www.cupertino.gov/saferoutes/msbs
events.blurb: You and your middle schooler learn safe biking fundamentals to ride to school confidently.

events.title: Family Friendly Introduction to Nature Journaling
events.description: Explore Mc Clellan Ranch Preserve through nature journaling. Participants will have an opportunity to develop keen observation skills and learn more about the natural world through journaling. Learn the fundamentals of nature journaling and practice sketching and writing about what you see in the preserve. Connect with nature, develop your artistic skills, cultivate a sense of mindfulness, and appreciate the beauty of nature journaling as a community in this family-friendly experience. Open to a variety of experience levels. Children 14 and under must be accompanied by a parent or guardian who is also registered for the class. $32 Resident | $44 Non-resident Register
events.blurb: Sketch and jot notes as you learn nature journaling basics while observing McClellan Ranch Preserve.

events.title: Pooch Plunge
events.address: Blackberry Farm Park, 21979 San Fernando Avenue, Cupertino, CA, 95014
events.description: Let your pup take the plunge during a dog-only swim in the Blackberry Farm pools. It's a paws-itively fun way for your pooch to enjoy the pools before they open for summer swimming—tail wags are highly encouraged. Swim Sessions (45 minutes of tail-wagging fun) Block 1 - 9 to 9:45 a.m. Block 2 - 10 to 10:45 a.m. Block 3 - 11 to 11:45 a.m. Block 4 - Noon to 12:45 p.m. Register Registration opens in February for the spring event and in June for the fall event. For additional information, visit the Pooch Plunge webpage.
events.blurb: Your dog swims solo in the pool during a dog-only splash session before summer opens.

events.title: Join-A-Ranger: Night Hike and Flashlight Tour
events.description: Enjoy a fun-filled Night Hike and Flashlight Tour of Mc Clellan Ranch Preserve and Blackberry Farm! We will explore the woods and nature at night, learn about nocturnal animals' special adaptations, and test our heightened senses in the dark with games and activities. The hike is approximately one mile on a combination of dirt trails and paved paths. Please wear appropriate walking shoes and a warm jacket and bring a flashlight or head-lamp. All attendees must register and children must be accompanied by an adult. $10 Resident | $13 Non-resident Register
events.blurb: Grab a flashlight and explore Blackberry Farm's woods after dark, learning how nocturnal animals adapt.

events.title: Watershed Habitat of the Steelhead Nature Walk
events.description: Join a Naturalist and learn about the Steelhead life cycle, riparian habitat and challenges faced in Stevens Creek. We will walk through history and view our ecosystem through the lens of this phenomenal species. You and your child will identify aquatic insects as a measure of water quality. The hike is approximately one mile on a combination of dirt trails and paved paths. All attendees must register and children must be accompanied by an adult. $25 Resident | $32 Non-resident Register
events.blurb: Walk Stevens Creek with a naturalist and identify aquatic insects to learn about the steelhead's life cycle.

events.title: Sweet Pea and Lil' Ninjas Open House
events.address: Monta Vista Recreation Center, 22601 Voss Rd., 95014
events.description: Climb, swing, balance, and play at the Sweet Pea Gymnastics and Lil' Ninjas Open House! Families can explore the Gymnastics Room, meet the Bay Aerials team, and learn about year-round programs for ages 1 to 12. Children will also have a chance to try the equipment and take on a few ninja obstacles. Join us Saturday, August 15, from 2 to 4 p.m. at Monta Vista Recreation Center. The event is free,. Rsvp at reg4rec.org using activity number 34168.
events.blurb: Climb, swing, and balance through the gym, meet the Bay Aerials team, and try out gymnastics equipment with the kids.

events.title: Join-A-Ranger: Bird Id and Guided Hike
events.description: The Park Rangers invite you to learn about the birds living in our riparian habitat and how to ID them! Walk the trails to see the environment they live in and maybe catch a glimpse of a Red-Shoulder Hawk, Screech Owl, or more! All attendees must register and an adult must accompany children. The hike is approximately one-quarter mile on a combination of dirt trails and paved paths. $10 Resident | $13 Non-resident Register
events.blurb: Walk the trails with a park ranger learning to identify local birds like red-shouldered hawks and screech owls.

events.title: The Employee Dharma Handbook
events.description: As an important rocket launch approaches, Val, an HR Executive at an aerospace company in Silicon Valley, suspects that a young engineer was overlooked for a promotion. Leela, a first generation Indian-American, doesn't really want to talk about it, but Val suspects sexism is the cause. What they don't expect to uncover are simmering tensions of ancient origins among the company's Indian immigrant employees…sorry, Valued Team Members. Soon Val is navigating a funhouse of identity, nepotism, and desire as she tries to understand the culture and biases of Leela's managers. When push comes to shove, Val will need to decide what her duty is to Leela, to the company, and to herself.
events.blurb: Watch a play about an HR executive navigating workplace bias at an aerospace company during a pivotal rocket launch.

events.title: Alice, Formerly of Wonderland
events.description: Seven-time Emmy Award-winning writer Mark Saltzman illuminates the beautiful coming of age story revealing the truth behind the fairytale between Alice Liddell—the real-life inspiration of Lewis Carroll's classic tale—and Prince Leopold, Queen Victoria's youngest son. Brimming with wit and emotional spark, this acapella musical weaves between Victorian reality and Wonderland whimsy, revealing a tender connection shaped by duty, distance, and the ache of what might have been. Perfect for the holiday season, Alice, Formerly of Wonderland glows with warmth, longing, and just enough wonder to feel like a gift.
events.blurb: Hear Emmy-winning writer Mark Saltzman reveal the real story between Alice Liddell and Prince Leopold.

Brimming with wit and emotional spark, this acapella musical weaves between Victorian reality and Wonderland whimsy, revealing a tender connection shaped by duty, distance, and the ache of what might have been. Perfect for the holiday season, Alice, Formerly of Wonderland glows with warmth, longing, and just enough wonder to feel like a gift.
events.blurb: Hear Emmy-winning writer Mark Saltzman reveal the real story between Alice Liddell and Prince Leopold.

events.title: Join-A-Ranger: Wildlife Tracking
events.description: Join a Park Ranger for a fun-filled wildlife tracking class. You'll learn about species identification through their tracks and sign-tracking. Become a nature detective and learn to read the ground like a storybook through the art of tracking. Please wear sturdy walking shoes and long pants that can get dirty. All attendees must register and children must be accompanied by an adult.
events.blurb: Learn to identify animal tracks and read the ground like a nature detective with a park ranger.

events.title: Dracula
events.venue: Mountain View Center for the Performing Arts
events.description: Sink your teeth into the ultimate Halloween thrill. Steven Dietz's adaptation of the timeless classic seduces with elegance and suspense, turning the legendary vampire tale into a high-octane theatrical ride. Bold, chilling, and wickedly smart, the story draws you into a world where reason clashes with superstition and love becomes dangerously intoxicating. This Dracula keeps you leaning forward—never quite sure who holds the power, or what darkness waits just out of sight.
events.blurb: Witness the gothic horror classic rendered as a compelling stage production with talented actors.

events.title: Join-A-Ranger: IDs for the Trees
events.description: Explore Mc Clellan Ranch Preserve alongside one of our Park Rangers. You'll learn how to use a dichotomous key to identify the different trees of the area and their uses. All attendees must register and an adult must accompany children. The hike is approximately one-quarter mile on a combination of dirt trails and paved paths. $10 Resident | $13 Non-resident Register
events.blurb: Learn to use a dichotomous key to identify trees on a quarter-mile hike with a park ranger.

events.title: Na Leo Mokupuni in Cupertino
events.description: Na Leo Mokupuni in Cupertino – meaning island sounds in Cupertino Experience the rhythms, songs, and dances of the islands! Na Leo Mokupuni brings together performers to celebrate the vibrant traditions of island music and dance—right here in Cupertino. Preregistration is recommended. Limited tickets available at the door, while supplies last.
events.blurb: Experience island rhythms, songs, and dances rooted in Hawaiian and Pacific island traditions.

events.title: Breakfast with Santa
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 North Stelling Road, 95014
events.description: You and your family are invited to a festive breakfast celebration with Santa! Enjoy delicious North Pole pancakes prepared by Santa's elves, take photos with Santa, and explore a holiday workshop filled with crafts and activities for children. Three seatings: 8:30 a.m., 9:30 a.m., 10:30 a.m. Register Registration opens in late July.
events.blurb: Enjoy pancakes with Santa, take photos, and do holiday crafts with your family.

events.title: Monster Mash
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 North Stelling Road, Cupertino, CA, 95014
events.description: Get ready for a fang-tastic good time at Cupertino Parks and Recreation's annual Monster Mash Halloween event at the Quinlan Community Center. Put on your favorite costume and enjoy an evening of games, trick-or-treating, spooky crafts, and pumpkin decorating. It's a fun-filled night of Halloween activities for the whole family.
events.blurb: Wear your costume and enjoy games, trick-or-treating, spooky crafts, and pumpkin decorating.

events.title: 2026 Cupertino Community Service Awards
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 N. Stelling Road, 95014
events.description: The third annual Cupertino Community Service Awards will take place on Wednesday, October 28. This ceremony will recognize the 2026 Crest Awards (Cupertino Recognizes Extra Steps Taken), and Commissioners who served on a City Commission. More details and registration information for the October 28 event will be available soon.
events.blurb: Watch the Cupertino Community Service Awards ceremony honoring local volunteers and commissioners.

events.title: Environmental Education Center Open House
events.description: Drop by the Environmental Education Center (EEC) at Mc Clellan Ranch Preserve every Saturday from 11 a.m. to 3 p.m. for family fun and discovery. Meet the City's animal ambassadors, explore local wildlife, engage with preserve staff and park rangers, and enjoy hands-on activities that bring nature to life.
events.blurb: Meet animal ambassadors, learn about local wildlife, and do hands-on activities with park rangers.

events.title: Signing Santa
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 North Stelling Road, 95014
events.description: Deaf and hard of hearing children, along with their families, are invited to spend time with a Santa who understands sign language! Santa only communicates using sign language but an interpreter will be on-site to help translate for friends and family. Register Registration opens in late July.
events.blurb: Meet a signing Santa who communicates entirely in ASL with your deaf or hard-of-hearing children.

events.title: Free Shakespeare in the Park Event
events.description: Free Shakespeare in the Park brings you Anthony and Cleopatra directed by Katja Rivera. Set against the backdrop of the Roman Empire and Egypt, For show details, Anthony and Cleopatra examines the conflict between duty and desire, and the impact of personal relationships. Visit sfshakes.org.
events.blurb: Watch Shakespeare's Anthony and Cleopatra performed outdoors in the amphitheater.

events.title: Guided Meditation & Breath Awareness
events.address: Central Park Library - Redwood Room, 2635 Homestead Road, Santa Clara, California 95051
events.description: A beginner-friendly guided meditation and breath awareness session designed to help participants relax and reconnect with the present moment. The program includes guided meditation, gentle joint rotations, and light movements to support relaxation. No registration required.
events.blurb: Join a beginner-friendly guided meditation and breath awareness session.

events.title: Tree Lighting
events.address: Quinlan Community Center, 10185 North Stelling Road, Cupertino, CA, 95014
events.description: Kick off the holiday season at the City's annual Community Tree Lighting! Enjoy the moment the tree is illuminated with hundreds of twinkling lights, live music from a local choir, and festive holiday cheer. After the tree lighting, families can visit Santa and take photos.
events.blurb: Watch the holiday tree light up, hear a local choir sing, and visit Santa for photos.

events.title: Don Giovanni
events.description: DON Giovanni Back after 12 years, Opera San Jose builds a new production of Mozart's dark psychological thriller. Seduction, rebellion, and supernatural justice collide in a thrilling look at a notorious libertine's downfall. The production follows a charismatic anti-hero who pursues pleasure without remorse, leaving a trail of broken hearts and vengeful spirits in a […]
events.blurb: Watch Mozart's dark psychological thriller about a libertine's seduction, rebellion, and supernatural downfall.

events.title: Health Talk - Self-Care Across Your Lifespan
events.description: Join Dr. Gary Huang, an adult, child & adolescent psychiatrist at Kaiser Permanente, for a discussion about lifelong self-care tips. Learn mindfulness and stress management techniques, best practices for restorative sleep, and explore the 6 pillars of Lifestyle Medicine.
events.blurb: Learn mindfulness, sleep strategies, and lifestyle medicine principles from psychiatrist Dr. Gary Huang.

events.title: Baer Blacksmith Shop Demonstrations
events.description: Community members are invited to visit the Baer Blacksmith Shop from 11 a.m. to 3 p.m. and learn about early Cupertino history, the craft of blacksmithing, and learn from our experienced blacksmith, who will be firing the forge to give demonstrations.
events.blurb: Watch a blacksmith fire the forge and demonstrate the craft while learning early Cupertino history.
